text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful approach is to set SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This helps focus efforts and measure progress effectively. To stay on track, break the project into milestones—key points that mark significant progress. Each milestone should lead to a deliverable, such as a report or prototype, which provides tangible proof of work. It is also important to ensure stakeholder alignment, meaning everyone involved agrees on the goals and expectations. Without this, the project may face confusion or delays. By combining SMART goals, milestones, deliverables, and aligned stakeholders, your project is more likely to succeed.
